codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the span unwrapping bug by clearing the class attribute on spans whose all explicit styles were stripped as fully inherited. The implementation is minimal (13 lines added, 1 file changed), well-reasoned, and properly integrates with the existing span unwrapping flow. The root cause was that the stripping loop cleared the style attribute but left the class attribute intact, preventing the parent-level span unwrapping pass from detecting and removing the no-op span.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md states '10 pre-existing test failures' but this fix reduced failures to 8. The baseline failure count in AGENTS.md should be updated to reflect the new baseline (8) so subsequent workers have accurate expectations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line: 'Current baseline: 10 pre-existing test failures — do not regress (was 12, improved)'. Handoff reports: '8 FAIL lines (down from 10 baseline, no regressions)'. Subsequent commit 7754074 may have further changed this.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The span unwrapping in simplifyStylesElementFull has a two-phase design: (1) children are processed recursively first (stripping happens per-child), then (2) the parent checks for no-op spans with empty class+style+attrs. This ordering dependency is subtle and not documented. Future workers modifying the stripping or unwrapping logic need to understand this ordering to avoid regressions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Lines 1416-1429: recursive child processing. Lines 1432-1451: parent-level span unwrapping check. Lines 1535-1649: child-level stripping (runs during recursive processing). The fix at lines 1638-1649 correctly places class clearing after setElementStyleString, so it takes effect before the parent's span unwrapping check.

summary: PASS. The fix is correct and minimal. It adds 13 lines to clear the class attribute on spans whose explicitStyle becomes empty after the inheritance stripping loop. This allows the existing parent-level span unwrapping pass (which checks for empty class, style, and attrs) to correctly remove no-op spans. Both target tests pass, failures reduced from 10 to 8, and all 4 diff counts remain at baseline with no regressions. No bugs or edge cases were missed — the fix correctly handles: (1) spans with class-only attributes, (2) spans with nil Attrs, (3) spans with other non-style attributes that should prevent unwrapping. The worker followed the skill procedure correctly.
